data.description: Inserts the new user's email and hashed password into accounts_database.
data.name: postgres action
data.label: Insert User

executeQueryParams.query: INSERT INTO accounts_database (email, password)  VALUES ('{{outputs.Signup Trigger.success.body.email}}', '{{outputs.Hash Password.success.hash}}')

data.description: Queries accounts_database to check if the email is already registered.
data.name: postgres action
data.label: Check Email

executeQueryParams.query: SELECT * FROM accounts_database WHERE email = '{{outputs.Signup Trigger.success.body.email}}'

data.description: If rowCount == 0, email is free to register (True). Otherwise rejects (False).
data.name: if-else
data.label: Email Available?

data.description: Returns { success: false, error: "Email already exists" } to the frontend.
data.name: respond-to-webhook
data.label: Email Exists Response

data.description: Queries accounts_database for a row matching both email and hashed password.
data.name: postgres action
data.label: Verify Credentials

executeQueryParams.query: SELECT * FROM accounts_database  WHERE email = '{{outputs.Login Trigger.success.body.email}}'  AND password = '{{outputs.Hash Password.success.hash}}'

data.description: If rowCount > 0, credentials match (True). Otherwise rejects (False).
data.name: if-else
data.label: Credentials Valid?

data.description: Returns { success: false, error: "Invalid email or password" } to the frontend.
data.name: respond-to-webhook
data.label: Invalid Credentials Response

executeQueryParams.query: SELECT * FROM courses WHERE is_default = true UNION SELECT c.* FROM courses c JOIN user_courses uc ON uc.course_id = c.id WHERE uc.user_id = '{{outputs.CoursesWebhook.success.query.userId}}'

data.description: Executes custom JavaScript code on the input data.
data.name: function
data.label: ParseCourseJSON

parameters.code:
let raw = outputs.GenerateCourseContent.success
raw = raw.replace(/```json/g, '').replace(/```/g, '').replace(/`/g, "'").trim()
const parsed = JSON.parse(raw)
return parsed

data.description: Checks if a course with the given topic already exists in the database before generating a new one.
data.name: postgres action
data.label: InsertCourse

executeQueryParams.query: INSERT INTO courses (title, description, tag, icon, tag_color, tag_bg, drive_link, is_default) VALUES (   '{{outputs.ParseCourseJSON.success.title}}',   '{{outputs.ParseCourseJSON.success.description}}',   '{{outputs.ParseCourseJSON.success.tag}}',   '{{outputs.ParseCourseJSON.success.icon}}',   '{{outputs.ParseCourseJSON.success.tag_color}}',   '{{outputs.ParseCourseJSON.success.tag_bg}}',   '{{outputs.BuildDriveLink.success.drive_link}}',   false ) RETURNING id

data.description: Executes custom JavaScript code on the input data.
data.name: function
data.label: BuildPrompt

parameters.code:
const topic = outputs.GenerateCourseWebhook.success.body.topic
return {
  prompt: `You are an expert course creator. Generate a structured course on the topic: "${topic}"

Return ONLY a valid JSON object with no extra text, no markdown, no backticks. The JSON must have exactly these fields:

{
  "title": "use exactly this as the title: ${topic}",
  "description": "a one sentence description of the course",
  "tag": "a short category label (e.g. AI, Dev, Security, Networks, IoT, Data, Web)",
  "icon": "a single relevant emoji",
  "tag_color": "a hex color code for the tag text",
  "tag_bg": "a lighter hex color code for the tag background",
  "content": "the full course content with this exact structure:\\n# [Title]\\n## Introduction\\n[2-3 paragraphs of explanation]\\n## Section 1: [Title]\\n[1 paragraph explaining the concept, then bullet points with details]\\n## Section 2: [Title]\\n[1 paragraph explaining the concept, then bullet points with details]\\n## Section 3: [Title]\\n[1 paragraph explaining the concept, then bullet points with details]\\n## Section 4: [Title]\\n[1 paragraph explaining the concept, then bullet points with details]\\n## Section 5: [Title]\\n[1 paragraph explaining the concept, then bullet points with details]\\n## Conclusion\\n[2-3 paragraphs]"
}`
}
